get.description: Retrieve a list of all events. Supports filtering by status and managerId. Accessible by eventmanager, client, osc, attendee, superuser.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status. Omit or leave empty to return all events.

parameters.name: managerId
parameters.description: Filter by manager user ID (e.g. u1, u2). Pass a user ID, not a role name.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: User role (superuser | eventmanager | client | osc | attendee)

post.description: Accessible by eventmanager and client. Superuser can also create events.

403.description: Only eventmanager or client can create events

get.description: Returns aggregate stats: total events, RSVPs, ratings, engagement scores.

parameters.name: managerId
parameters.description: Filter stats for a specific manager

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: User role (superuser | eventmanager | client | osc | attendee)

put.description: Full update of an event. Eventmanager and client only.

patch.description: Update specific fields. Eventmanager and client only. Use for status changes (e.g. pending → live).

delete.description: Permanently remove an event. Eventmanager only (or superuser).

get.description: List all users. Eventmanager or superuser only. Optionally filter by role.

post.description: Create a new user account. Eventmanager or superuser only.

get.description: Accessible by superuser, eventmanager, client, and OSC.

post.description: Attendee registers for an event. Phone must be exactly 10 digits.

get.description: Attendee fetches their own RSVPs by email query param.

patch.description: Update RSVP details or status. Attendees can cancel their own; OSC/eventmanager/client can update any.

post.description: OSC scanner endpoint — validates and marks ticket as attended.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Must be: superuser | osc | eventmanager | client

get.description: Eventmanager views all client event hosting requests.

post.description: Client submits a request to host an event.

patch.description: Eventmanager reviews a pending request. Approval requires managerId.
patch.summary: Approve or reject an event request

post.description: OSC staff or client reports an issue; creates a notification for eventmanager.
Assign a new OSC staff member to an event. Eventmanager or client.

get.summary: Get team statistics for an event

patch.description: OSC can update their own status; eventmanager/client can change any field.

get.description: Returns polls, optionally filtered by event or status. Accessible by client, eventmanager, osc, attendee, and superuser.

post.description: Client or eventmanager creates a poll for an event. Requires at least 2 options.

patch.description: Client or eventmanager can update a poll. Updating options resets vote counts.

delete.description: Client or eventmanager permanently removes a poll.

get.description: Returns vote counts and percentage breakdown per option.
get.summary: Get poll results with vote percentages

post.description: Attendee votes on an open poll. Each attendee (by email) can vote only once per poll.

400.description: Poll is closed or invalid option

patch.description: Client or eventmanager closes the poll.

get.description: Returns all questions, optionally filtered by eventId or status. Accessible by client, eventmanager, osc, attendee, and superuser.

post.description: Attendee submits a question for the client/eventmanager to answer.
post.summary: Ask a question about an event

get.description: Attendee fetches all questions they have asked across events.
Returns total, answered, and unanswered counts.
get.summary: Get Q&A statistics for an event

patch.description: Attendee can edit their own question text; client/eventmanager can also update.

delete.description: Client or eventmanager permanently removes a Q&A entry.

patch.description: Client or eventmanager provides an answer to an attendee question.

info.title: WEvents API
info.description:
## Event Management System REST API

This API powers the WEvents platform, supporting **five distinct roles** in a clear hierarchy:

| Role | Header Value | Hierarchy | Description |
|------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| **superuser** | `role: superuser` | 🔴 Top | Platform Administrator — unrestricted access to ALL endpoints |
| **eventmanager** | `role: eventmanager` | 🟠 2nd | Event Manager — full CRUD on events, approves/rejects requests |
| **client** | `role: client` | 🟡 3rd | Client — creates events, manages polls, answers attendee Q&A |
| **osc** | `role: osc` | 🟢 4th | On-Site Coordinator — manages team, scanner, reports for assigned event |
| **attendee** | `role: attendee` | 🔵 5th | Attendee — browse events, RSVP, ask questions, vote on polls |

### Role Privilege Summary
- **superuser**: Bypasses all role checks — can call any API regardless of the roles listed
- **eventmanager**: Full event lifecycle management, user management, request approvals
- **client**: Create events, manage polls, answer attendee questions, view event interactions
- **osc**: Team management, check-in scanning, notifications for their assigned event
- **attendee**: Browse events, RSVP, submit questions, vote on polls

### Authentication
All protected endpoints require a **`role`** header. Pass the role string directly in the request header:
```
role: eventmanager
```

### New in v2.0
- 🆕 **Polls API** (`/polls`): Client creates polls, attendees vote, results with percentages
- 🆕 **Q&A API** (`/qna`): Attendees ask questions, client/eventmanager answers them
- 🆕 **client** role: Creates events, polls, and manages event interactions
- 🔄 **superuser** renamed to **eventmanager** (superuser is now platform-level)
- 🔄 **enduser** renamed to **attendee**

### Data Management
All data is managed **in-memory** (no external database). Data resets when the server restarts.

role-header.name: role
role-header.description: User role (superuser | eventmanager | client | osc | attendee)

type.description: Session type (keynote, panel, workshop, talk, demo, break)

description.example: Explore the future of technology with industry leaders.

phone.description: Phone number — must be exactly 10 digits

description.example: Annual technology conference featuring AI and blockchain workshops

managerId.description: Manager ID to assign (required when approving)

desc.example: QR scanner at Gate 2 is offline

eventId.description: Event ID they are assigned to
Event ID this poll belongs to

question.example: Which session did you enjoy most?

createdBy.description: Client ID who created the poll

option.description: The option the attendee is voting for

eventId.description: Event ID the question belongs to

question.example: What is the dress code for the event?
question.description: The question asked by the attendee

askedBy.description: Email of the attendee asking the question

answer.description: The answer provided by the client or eventmanager

answeredBy.description: ID of the user providing the answer

question.example: Is parking available at the venue?
